children.text: “administrator” means the person or persons described in paragraph (16) of ;section 1002 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: who are contributing sponsors of the plan in such plan year,

children.label: B
children.text: who, at any time during such plan year, are members of the same controlled group, and

children.label: i
children.text: the two immediately preceding plan years, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the first two of the three immediately preceding plan years,

children.label: A
children.text: to which more than one employer is required to contribute,

children.label: B
children.text: which is maintained pursuant to one or more collective bargaining agreements between one or more employee organizations and more than one employer, and

children.label: C
children.text: which satisfies such other requirements as the Secretary of Labor may prescribe by regulation,

children.label: i
children.text: a plan shall be considered a multiemployer plan on and after its termination date if the plan was a multiemployer plan under this paragraph for the plan year preceding such termination, and

children.label: ii
children.text: for any plan year which began before , the term “multiemployer plan” means a plan described in as in effect immediately before such date;September 26, 1980section 414(f) of title 26
“corporation”, except where the context clearly requires otherwise, means the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation established under ;section 1302 of this title
“fund” means the appropriate fund established under ;section 1305 of this title

“basic benefits” means benefits guaranteed under (other than under section 1322(c) of this title), or under (other than under );section 1322 of this title1section 1322a of this titlesection 1322a(g) of this titleSee References in Text note below.1
“non-basic benefits” means benefits guaranteed under section 1322(c) of this title or 1322a(g) of this title;1

“nonforfeitable benefit” means, with respect to a plan, a benefit for which a participant has satisfied the conditions for entitlement under the plan or the requirements of this chapter (other than submission of a formal application, retirement, completion of a required waiting period, or death in the case of a benefit which returns all or a portion of a participant’s accumulated mandatory employee contributions upon the participant’s death), whether or not the benefit may subsequently be reduced or suspended by a plan amendment, an occurrence of any condition, or operation of this chapter or title 26;
Repealed. , , .Pub. L. 113–235, div. O, title I, § 108(a)(3)(A)128 Stat. 2787Dec. 16, 2014

children.label: A
children.text: the plan’s joint board of trustees, or

children.label: B
children.text: if the plan has no joint board of trustees, the plan administrator;

“contribution base unit” means a unit with respect to which an employer has an obligation to contribute under a multiemployer plan, as defined in regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury;

“outstanding claim for withdrawal liability” means a plan’s claim for the unpaid balance of the liability determined under part 1 of subtitle E for which demand has been made, valued in accordance with regulations prescribed by the corporation;

“contributing sponsor”, of a single-employer plan, means a person described in (without regard to ) or (without regard to section 412(b)(2) of such title).section 1082(b)(1) of this titlesection 1082(b)(2) of this titlesection 412(b)(1) of title 262So in original. The period probably should be a semicolon.2

children.label: A
children.text: “controlled group” means, in connection with any person, a group consisting of such person and all other persons under common control with such person;

children.label: B
children.text: the determination of whether two or more persons are under “common control” shall be made under regulations of the corporation which are consistent and coextensive with regulations prescribed for similar purposes by the Secretary of the Treasury under subsections (b) and (c) of ; andsection 414 of title 26

children.label: i
children.text: notwithstanding any other provision of this subchapter, during any period in which an individual possesses, directly or indirectly, the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of an affected air carrier of which he was an accountable owner, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise, the affected air carrier shall be considered to be under common control not only with those persons described in subparagraph (B), but also with all related persons; and

children.label: I
children.text: “affected air carrier” means an air carrier, as defined in , that holds a certificate of public convenience and necessity under for route number 147, as of ;section 40102(a)(2) of title 49section 41102 of title 49November 12, 1991

children.label: II
children.text: “related person” means any person which was under common control (as determined under subparagraph (B)) with an affected air carrier on , or any successor to such related person;October 10, 1991

children.label: III
children.text: “accountable owner” means any individual who on , owned directly or indirectly through the application of more than 50 percent of the total voting power of the stock of an affected air carrier;October 10, 1991section 318 of title 26

children.label: IV
children.text: “successor” means any person that acquires, directly or indirectly through the application of , more than 50 percent of the total voting power of the stock of a related person, more than 50 percent of the total value of the securities (as defined in ) of the related person, more than 50 percent of the total value of the assets of the related person, or any person into which such related person shall be merged or consolidated; andsection 318 of title 26section 1002(20) of this title

children.label: V
children.text: “individual” means a living human being;
“single-employer plan” means any defined benefit plan (as defined in ) which is not a multiemployer plan;section 1002(35) of this title
“benefit liabilities” means the benefits of employees and their beneficiaries under the plan (within the meaning of );section 401(a)(2) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: the actuarial present value (determined as of such date on the basis of assumptions prescribed by the corporation for purposes of ) of the benefits of the participant or beneficiary under the plan which are guaranteed under , oversection 1344 of this titlesection 1322 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the current value (as of such date) of the assets of the plan which are required to be allocated to those benefits under ;section 1344 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the value of the benefit liabilities under the plan (determined as of such date on the basis of assumptions prescribed by the corporation for purposes of ), oversection 1344 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the current value (as of such date) of the assets of the plan;

children.label: A
children.text: the value of the benefit liabilities under the plan (determined as of the termination date on the basis of assumptions prescribed by the corporation for purposes of ), oversection 1344 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the value of the benefit liabilities which would be so determined by only taking into account benefits which are guaranteed under or to which assets of the plan are allocated under ;section 1322 of this titlesection 1344 of this title
“person” has the meaning set forth in ;section 1002(9) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: each participant in the plan,

children.label: B
children.text: each beneficiary under the plan who is a beneficiary of a deceased participant or who is an alternate payee (within the meaning of ) under an applicable qualified domestic relations order (within the meaning of ),section 1056(d)(3)(K) of this titlesection 1056(d)(3)(B)(i) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: each employee organization representing participants in the plan, and

children.label: D
children.text: the corporation,

An individual who owns the entire interest in an unincorporated trade or business is treated as his own employer, and a partnership is treated as the employer of each partner who is an employee within the meaning of . For purposes of this subchapter, under regulations prescribed by the corporation, all employees of trades or businesses (whether or not incorporated) which are under common control shall be treated as employed by a single employer and all such trades and businesses as a single employer. The regulations prescribed under the preceding sentence shall be consistent and coextensive with regulations prescribed for similar purposes by the Secretary of the Treasury under .section 401(c)(1) of title 26section 414(c) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: except as otherwise provided in subtitle E, contributions or other payments shall be considered made under a plan for a plan year if they are made within the period prescribed under section 412(c)(10) of title 26 (determined, in the case of a terminated plan, as if the plan had continued beyond the termination date), and3See References in Text note below.3

children.label: B
children.text: the term “Secretary of the Treasury” means the Secretary of the Treasury or such Secretary’s delegate.
